commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh And I will strike the winter house with the summer house; and the houses of ivory shall perish, and the great houses shall have an end, says the LORD. Septuagint, "winged house "to keep off cold, (St. Jerome) or to give air. (Calmet) Summer-house. The noblemen had such is cooler regions. (Menochius) The kings of Persia passed the summer at Ecbatana. (Xenophon, Cyrop. ) Palladius (i. ) orders that the summer apartments must look to the north. Many ornaments of this nature appeared in them, (Calmet) whence Achab's palace was so called, 3 Kings xxii.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
